_meta.description: Africa subset of the retirement city dataset, structured to match the master schema. All scores are 1-10 (10 = best). Monthly costs in USD for a single person, comfortable lifestyle.
_meta.modeled_on: Lisbon (id 2 in master dataset): a safe, coastal, developed city with strong healthcare, good internet, an established expat community, and retiree-friendly visa access. Cape Town and Marrakech are the closest Africa analogues.

schema_notes.scores: All *_score fields are 1-10 integers. 10 = best/most favorable for retirees.
schema_notes.monthly_cost: USD, single person, comfortable lifestyle (not budget, not luxury).
schema_notes.visa_ease_score: 10 = easy long-stay visa or retirement visa available. 1 = very restrictive. Morocco, South Africa, and Mauritius offer the most accessible long-stay or retirement pathways. Many sub-Saharan nations have limited formal retirement visa programs.
schema_notes.healthcare_score: 10 = world-class private hospitals, low cost. 1 = poor infrastructure.
schema_notes.safety_score: 10 = very safe. 1 = high crime or instability.
schema_notes.climate_score: 10 = year-round pleasant. Subjective; filter by climate_type.
schema_notes.overall_retirement_score: Weighted composite. Recalculate when user adjusts priority weights.
schema_notes.ids: Integer ids here are local to this Africa file (1-60). The master table uses UUID primary keys, so renumber or drop on import as needed.

_meta.supabase_table_sql: CREATE TABLE retirement_cities (id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(), city TEXT NOT NULL, country TEXT NOT NULL, region TEXT NOT NULL, subregion TEXT, latitude FLOAT, longitude FLOAT, monthly_cost_usd INTEGER, lifestyle_label TEXT, description TEXT, tags TEXT[], visa_ease_score INTEGER, healthcare_score INTEGER, safety_score INTEGER, climate_score INTEGER, expat_community_score INTEGER, internet_score INTEGER, overall_retirement_score FLOAT, climate_type TEXT, language TEXT, currency TEXT, time_zone TEXT, popular_neighborhoods TEXT[], visa_notes TEXT, healthcare_notes TEXT, best_for TEXT[], avoid_if TEXT[], expense_shares JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW());

cities.description: The 'Red City' — a sensory explosion of souks, riads, and the legendary Djemaa el-Fna square, with a booming international expat scene and superb value for money.
cities.visa_notes: Morocco's long-stay visa allows 1-year renewable residence with proof of income. No formal retirement visa, but the process is well-established and commonly used by European retirees.
cities.healthcare_notes: Clinique du Sud and Polyclinique du Sud provide adequate private care; Casablanca for complex specialist needs.
best_for: Culture and arts lovers
Those wanting Europe-adjacent exotic living
Riad lifestyle enthusiasts and foodies
avoid_if: Those sensitive to summer heat (very hot July–August)
Retirees wanting fast specialist healthcare on-site

cities.description: Morocco's cosmopolitan economic capital — Art Deco boulevards, the iconic Hassan II Mosque, the best private hospitals in the country, and a large French-speaking expat community.
cities.visa_notes: Morocco long-stay residence applies. Best city in Morocco for private healthcare access — a major factor for older retirees.
cities.healthcare_notes: Clinique Ghandi, Clinique Badr, and Institut Pasteur provide Morocco's best private medical care.
best_for: Health-conscious retirees wanting Morocco's best hospitals
Those wanting an international business-city lifestyle
French speakers
avoid_if: Those wanting Marrakech's romantic medina atmosphere
Retirees sensitive to big-city noise and traffic

cities.description: Morocco's elegant capital — a quieter, more European-feeling city than Marrakech, with UNESCO heritage medina, Atlantic coast access, and the country's best infrastructure.
cities.visa_notes: Morocco long-stay residence applies. Rabat's administrative status means smoother bureaucratic processes for residents.
cities.healthcare_notes: Clinique Universitaire de Rabat-Salé and Avicenne Military Hospital; quality private clinics in Agdal district.
best_for: Safety-first retirees wanting Morocco's most ordered city
Those wanting coastal Atlantic access with capital-city infrastructure
Francophone retirees
avoid_if: Those wanting Marrakech's vibrant medina energy
Beach lovers (Rabat's beaches are windier and cooler)

cities.description: The world's largest car-free urban area and medieval Islamic city — Fez el-Bali is a UNESCO medina of extraordinary labyrinthine complexity, tanneries, and ancient learning.
cities.visa_notes: Morocco long-stay residence applies. Cheaper than Marrakech; popular with riad-restoration expats.
cities.healthcare_notes: CHU Hassan II and private clinics in Ville Nouvelle; Casablanca for specialist care.
best_for: Medieval Islamic history enthusiasts
Riad renovation and restoration retirees
Those wanting the most authentic Moroccan medina experience
avoid_if: Those with mobility challenges (medina is entirely pedestrian and hilly)
Retirees wanting beach or coastal access

cities.description: Morocco's purpose-built beach resort city — a modern beachfront with 300+ days of sunshine, Atlantic surf, and Morocco's most accessible expat beach retirement lifestyle.
cities.visa_notes: Morocco long-stay residence applies. Agadir is Morocco's most popular beach destination for Northern European retirees.
cities.healthcare_notes: Polyclinique du Souss and Clinique Argana provide adequate private care; Casablanca for specialists.
best_for: Beach and sunshine lovers
Those wanting a modern, resort-style Moroccan city
Northern European retirees wanting reliable winter sun
avoid_if: Those wanting cultural richness of Fez or Marrakech
Retirees wanting a traditional medina experience

cities.description: The gateway between Europe and Africa — just 14km from Spain across the Strait of Gibraltar, with a storied literary history, Mediterranean-Atlantic beaches, and an emerging arts scene.
cities.visa_notes: Morocco long-stay residence applies. Ferry access to Spain makes it ideal for those maintaining European ties.
cities.healthcare_notes: Clinique Assaada and modern private clinics; Casablanca reachable by train for specialist needs.
best_for: Spain-proximity seekers (day trips possible)
Literary history enthusiasts (Bowles, Ginsberg, Burroughs)
Those wanting Mediterranean climate with Morocco prices
avoid_if: Those wanting hot desert sun (Tangier is windier and cooler)
Retirees wanting a large settled expat community

cities.description: One of humanity's great cities — the Pyramids, the Egyptian Museum, the Nile, and 20 million people in a sprawling ancient metropolis at incredibly low USD costs.
cities.visa_notes: Egypt's Investor Residency and annual tourist visa extensions are commonly used; no formal retirement visa. USD-denominated living is extremely cheap post-devaluation.
cities.healthcare_notes: As-Salam International Hospital and Cleopatra Hospital provide quality private care; Maadi district has many English-speaking clinics.
best_for: History and archaeology enthusiasts
Those wanting world-class ancient civilization immersion
Budget retirees wanting Middle Eastern/African urban life
avoid_if: Those sensitive to traffic chaos and air pollution
Retirees wanting a comfortable, low-density lifestyle

cities.description: The legendary Mediterranean city of Cleopatra and the ancient Library — a long Corniche seafront, Greek-Roman heritage, and a cooler, breezier alternative to Cairo.
cities.visa_notes: Egypt visa situation applies. Alexandria's Corniche lifestyle is popular with Egyptian domestic retirees; international presence is smaller.
cities.healthcare_notes: Alexandria University hospitals and private clinics adequate; Cairo for complex cases.
best_for: History lovers wanting a Mediterranean Egyptian city
Those wanting a cooler coastal alternative to Cairo
Budget retirees wanting seafront living
avoid_if: Those wanting a significant expat community
Retirees wanting beach-resort amenities (Alexandria is a lived-in city, not a resort)

cities.description: The world's greatest open-air museum — Karnak, the Valley of the Kings, and Hatshepsut's temple surround a tranquil Nile city at rock-bottom prices.
cities.visa_notes: Egypt visa situation applies. Luxor has a small but established expat community drawn by the archaeology.
cities.healthcare_notes: Luxor International Hospital; Cairo (1 hr flight) for specialist needs.
best_for: Archaeology devotees
Ultra-budget retirees
Those wanting unparalleled ancient history as daily backdrop
avoid_if: Anyone sensitive to extreme heat (summer temperatures 40-45°C)
Those needing reliable healthcare

cities.description: Egypt's most laid-back Nile city — Nubian culture, Philae Temple, Abu Simbel excursions, and a gentle pace on the river's edge at Egypt's most pleasant winter temperatures.
cities.visa_notes: Egypt visa situation applies. Aswan's pleasanter winter climate makes it a popular short-stay destination.
cities.healthcare_notes: Aswan University Hospital; very limited private options — Luxor or Cairo for serious care.
best_for: Nubian culture enthusiasts
Those wanting Egypt's most peaceful Nile setting
Budget winter-sun seekers
avoid_if: Those needing healthcare infrastructure
Anyone visiting in summer (extreme heat)

cities.description: Egypt's Red Sea resort city — world-class diving and snorkeling, year-round sunshine, European-oriented resort infrastructure, and some of the cheapest beach living anywhere.
cities.visa_notes: Egypt visa situation applies. Hurghada and nearby El Gouna have large European (particularly German and Russian) expat communities.
cities.healthcare_notes: Hurghada International Hospital and El Gouna Hospital provide adequate care; Cairo for complex cases.
best_for: Divers and snorkelers
Budget beach retirees wanting Red Sea warmth
Europeans wanting cheap winter sun in a familiar-ish resort setting
avoid_if: Those wanting authentic Egyptian cultural immersion (very resort-focused)
Retirees needing specialist healthcare

cities.description: A purpose-built Red Sea diving resort at the tip of the Sinai Peninsula — extraordinary coral reefs, reliable sun, and well-developed tourist infrastructure at very low prices.
cities.visa_notes: Egypt visa situation applies. Sinai Entry Permit (free for Sharm only, 15 days) or full Egypt visa for longer stays.
cities.healthcare_notes: Sharm International Hospital and private dive-medical clinics; Cairo for serious cases.
best_for: Serious divers (Ras Mohammed, Blue Hole day trips)
Sun-maximizers
Budget beach resort retirees
avoid_if: Those wanting authentic cultural experience (purely resort town)
Safety-sensitive retirees (Sinai security concerns)

cities.description: A Mediterranean capital with one of Africa's finest medinas, Roman Carthage ruins nearby, beautiful beaches at La Marsa, and a sophisticated French-influenced café culture.
cities.visa_notes: Tunisia allows 3-month stays without a visa (for many nationalities) with extensions possible; a Residence Permit requires income proof. Process is manageable.
cities.healthcare_notes: Clinique Les Oliviers and Clinique El Amen provide solid private care at very low costs.
best_for: Francophone retirees
Mediterranean beach and history lovers
Budget retirees wanting European-adjacent North African culture
avoid_if: Those wanting political certainty (Tunisia has undergone political shifts)
Retirees wanting a large English-speaking community

cities.description: Tunisia's premier beach resort town — a beautiful medina, jasmine-scented gardens, Mediterranean beaches, and one of North Africa's most established European resort communities.
cities.visa_notes: Tunisia residence permit applies. Hammamet has hosted European retirees for decades; French and Italian speakers well-catered for.
cities.healthcare_notes: Local clinics adequate for routine care; Tunis (1 hr) for private hospitals.
best_for: European beach retirees wanting affordable Mediterranean living
Garden and jasmine culture lovers
Those wanting a relaxed Tunisian resort town
avoid_if: Those needing local hospital access
Retirees wanting urban energy or cultural depth

cities.description: A Mediterranean island of whitewashed villages, ancient synagogues, flamingo lagoons, and some of Tunisia's best beaches — peaceful, affordable, and deeply authentic.
cities.visa_notes: Tunisia residence permit applies. Island living with causeway connection to the mainland.
cities.healthcare_notes: Regional Hospital of Djerba; Sfax or Tunis for specialist care.
best_for: Island lifestyle devotees wanting authenticity at minimal cost
History and heritage tourism enthusiasts
Beach retirees wanting a quieter alternative to Hammamet
avoid_if: Those needing healthcare access
Retirees wanting modern urban infrastructure

cities.description: The 'White City' of the Mediterranean — a grand Ottoman and French colonial capital with a UNESCO Casbah, dramatic hillside setting, and a significant domestic but very limited international expat scene.
cities.visa_notes: Algeria's visa process is restrictive and bureaucratic. No retirement visa; long stays are very difficult to arrange for most nationalities. Not recommended for most retirees.
cities.healthcare_notes: Clinique El Azhar and some private facilities provide moderate care; medical tourism to France or Tunisia for serious cases.
best_for: Those with family or strong professional ties to Algeria
Adventurous retirees with high tolerance for bureaucracy
avoid_if: Almost all international retirees — visa restrictions and limited expat infrastructure make this extremely challenging

cities.description: Algeria's vibrant second city on the Mediterranean — famous for its Raï music heritage, Spanish colonial architecture, and a more relaxed pace than Algiers.
cities.visa_notes: Same Algeria restrictions apply. Oran has essentially no international expat retirement community.
cities.healthcare_notes: CHU d'Oran provides public care; private options limited.
best_for: Those with family ties or academic/professional connections to Algeria
avoid_if: All independent international retirees — Algeria's entry restrictions are a hard barrier

cities.description: Libya's Mediterranean capital has a remarkable Roman and Ottoman old city, but ongoing political instability makes it unsuitable for almost all international retirees at present.
cities.visa_notes: Libya is effectively inaccessible for most international visitors. No retirement visa. Active security concerns; Foreign Office of most nations advise against all travel. Not recommended.
cities.healthcare_notes: Healthcare infrastructure severely degraded by conflict; do not rely on local facilities.
best_for: Not currently suitable for retirement
avoid_if: All retirees — security situation makes long-term residence untenable for international nationals

cities.description: One of the world's most beautiful cities — Table Mountain, Boulders Beach penguins, the Winelands, and a world-class food and arts scene, all at favorable USD exchange rates.
cities.visa_notes: South Africa's Retirement Annuity Visa requires proof of pension income (~$1,500/month). Process is bureaucratic but established. Permanent residency path available.
cities.healthcare_notes: Groote Schuur Hospital (world-famous, performed first heart transplant) and private Netcare and Mediclinic hospitals provide excellent care.
best_for: Scenic beauty seekers
Wine and food lovers
Active outdoor retirees wanting mountains, ocean, and winelands combined
avoid_if: Safety-sensitive retirees (crime requires gated-community awareness)
Those wanting easy visa processes

cities.description: Africa's economic powerhouse — superb private hospitals, world-class restaurants, excellent shopping, and Kruger Park proximity, offset by the highest crime profile of South African cities.
cities.visa_notes: South Africa Retirement Annuity Visa applies. Sandton and northern suburbs are strongly secured and heavily expat-populated.
cities.healthcare_notes: Morningside Clinic (Netcare) and Milpark Hospital are among Africa's finest private hospitals — a major draw for health-conscious retirees.
best_for: Health-focused retirees wanting Africa's best hospitals
Safari enthusiasts wanting Kruger access
Those wanting maximum urban amenities in Africa
avoid_if: Safety-sensitive retirees (requires security-conscious lifestyle)
Those wanting walkable, low-density living

cities.description: Africa's busiest port and South Africa's warmest major city — a long subtropical beach promenade, Indian-influenced cuisine, surfing, and more affordable prices than Cape Town.
cities.visa_notes: South Africa Retirement Annuity Visa applies. Durban is significantly cheaper than Cape Town for property and everyday living.
cities.healthcare_notes: Netcare St. Augustine's and Mediclinic Durban provide quality private care.
best_for: Beach and surfing enthusiasts
Foodies drawn to South Africa's best Indian cuisine
Budget South Africa retirees
avoid_if: Those sensitive to heat and humidity (subtropical year-round)
Safety-conscious retirees (crime awareness required)

cities.description: South Africa's administrative capital — the 'Jacaranda City' turns purple in October, with a calmer pace than Johannesburg, excellent hospitals, and easy Kruger access.
cities.visa_notes: South Africa Retirement Annuity Visa applies. Pretoria has a large diplomatic and international community.
cities.healthcare_notes: Steve Biko Academic Hospital and Netcare Unitas are excellent private facilities.
best_for: Those wanting Johannesburg's hospital quality in a calmer setting
Diplomatic community connection seekers
Kruger National Park access
avoid_if: Those wanting coastal or beach living
Safety-sensitive retirees

cities.description: The 'Friendly City' on Algoa Bay — gateway to Addo Elephant Park, beautiful beaches, and a more relaxed and affordable coastal lifestyle than Cape Town.
cities.visa_notes: South Africa Retirement Annuity Visa applies. Significantly cheaper than Cape Town with similar beach access.
cities.healthcare_notes: Mediclinic Port Elizabeth and Greenacres Hospital provide good private care.
best_for: Budget South Africa beach retirees
Safari enthusiasts (Addo Elephant Park 45 min)
Those wanting a friendlier pace than Cape Town
avoid_if: Retirees wanting big-city amenities or a large expat community
Those wanting Cape Town's spectacular scenery

cities.description: South Africa's wine capital and oldest university town — oak-lined streets, Cape Dutch architecture, world-class wines, and the Winelands lifestyle 45 minutes from Cape Town.
cities.visa_notes: South Africa Retirement Annuity Visa applies. Cape Town proximity without Cape Town prices.
cities.healthcare_notes: Mediclinic Stellenbosch and proximity to Cape Town hospitals provide solid coverage.
best_for: Wine and food lovers
Those wanting Cape Winelands lifestyle in a charming small city
University-town energy seekers
avoid_if: Those wanting beach-front living
Retirees wanting maximum urban amenities

cities.description: The world's best land-based whale-watching destination — Southern Right Whales breach in Walker Bay from June–December, in a charming coastal village with spectacular cliff walks.
cities.visa_notes: South Africa Retirement Annuity Visa applies. Hermanus is already popular with South African domestic retirees.
cities.healthcare_notes: Hermanus Private Hospital; Cape Town (1.5 hrs) for comprehensive specialist care.
best_for: Nature and marine wildlife lovers
Those wanting a quiet, charming coastal South African village
Wine and outdoor enthusiasts (Hemel-en-Aarde wines)
avoid_if: Those needing specialist healthcare locally
Retirees wanting urban amenities or entertainment

cities.description: East Africa's most cosmopolitan capital — rhinos and giraffes in the city's national park, world-class restaurants, and Africa's largest expat community, at a comfortable altitude of 1,700m.
cities.visa_notes: Kenya's Special Residence Permit (Investment) or retirement residency requires income proof and investment. An eVisa system is in place for initial entry. Process manageable.
cities.healthcare_notes: Aga Khan University Hospital and Nairobi Hospital are among East Africa's finest private facilities — a key draw for expats.
best_for: Safari enthusiasts (Maasai Mara day trip possible)
Those wanting East Africa's best infrastructure and dining
NGO and international organization retirees
avoid_if: Safety-sensitive retirees (urban crime awareness required)
Those wanting beach lifestyle (Nairobi is inland)

cities.description: Kenya's coast city — Swahili culture, Arabic-Portuguese fort, white-sand beaches, and the turquoise Indian Ocean, with lower costs than Nairobi.
cities.visa_notes: Kenya residence permit applies. Mombasa's Diani Beach is a separate, more upscale expat enclave south of the city.
cities.healthcare_notes: Aga Khan Hospital Mombasa provides adequate private care; Nairobi for serious specialist needs.
best_for: Indian Ocean beach lovers
Swahili culture enthusiasts
Those wanting affordable coastal Kenya living
avoid_if: Those sensitive to heat and humidity (tropical year-round)
Retirees wanting Nairobi's urban sophistication

cities.description: A small Kenyan coastal town with an outsized Italian expat presence — beautiful beaches, coral reefs, deep-sea fishing, and a relaxed barefoot lifestyle.
cities.visa_notes: Kenya residence permit applies. Malindi has one of Africa's most established Italian retiree communities.
cities.healthcare_notes: Malindi District Hospital; serious cases go to Mombasa or Nairobi.
best_for: Italian speakers wanting a slice of Italy on the Indian Ocean
Fishing and diving enthusiasts
Laid-back beach retirees
avoid_if: Those needing reliable healthcare or modern amenities
Non-Italian speakers wanting a large English expat community (Italian dominates here)

cities.description: Tanzania's commercial capital on the Indian Ocean — a rapidly developing port city with access to Zanzibar by ferry, Serengeti flights, and a growing expat community.
cities.visa_notes: Tanzania's Residence Permit requires investment or employment proof. Retirement-specific pathways are less developed than Kenya's.
cities.healthcare_notes: Aga Khan Hospital Dar es Salaam and IST Clinic provide adequate private care; Nairobi for advanced specialist needs.
best_for: Those wanting Zanzibar access as a base
Safari and Serengeti enthusiasts
Development-sector retirees with regional ties
avoid_if: Those expecting major-city amenity standards
Retirees needing specialist medical care locally

cities.description: The safari capital of Africa — gateway to the Serengeti, Ngorongoro Crater, Kilimanjaro, and Mount Meru, with a pleasant highland climate and a well-established safari-industry expat community.
cities.visa_notes: Tanzania Residence Permit applies. Arusha's highland location (1,400m) gives a pleasant climate and escape from coastal heat.
cities.healthcare_notes: Arusha Lutheran Medical Centre and Mount Meru Hospital; Nairobi (4 hrs or 1 hr flight) for advanced care.
best_for: Safari and wildlife enthusiasts wanting perpetual access to Africa's greatest parks
Mountain climbers (Kilimanjaro and Meru)
Nature-first retirees
avoid_if: Those wanting urban sophistication or beach living
Retirees needing reliable specialist healthcare

cities.description: The 'Spice Island' — a UNESCO Stone Town of Omani-Arab-Indian architecture, powder-white beaches, turquoise Indian Ocean, and one of East Africa's most romantic settings.
cities.visa_notes: Tanzania Residence Permit applies (Zanzibar is a semi-autonomous region within Tanzania). Rising tourism is pushing property prices up.
cities.healthcare_notes: Mnazi Mmoja Hospital and Zanzibar Medical Group; serious cases evacuate to Dar es Salaam or Nairobi.
best_for: Beach and island lifestyle lovers
History and Swahili-Arab culture enthusiasts
Spice and scuba diving devotees
avoid_if: Those needing reliable healthcare
Budget retirees (beach areas command premium prices)
Those wanting urban amenities

cities.description: A hilly, vibrant East African capital of surprising energy — boda-bodas, rolex chapatis, Lake Victoria access, gorilla-trekking proximity, and an improving expat infrastructure.
cities.visa_notes: Uganda's Class G (Special Pass) or Dependent Pass are common long-stay routes; no formal retirement visa. East African Community passport holders have freedom of movement.
cities.healthcare_notes: International Hospital Kampala (IHK) and Case Medical Centre provide adequate private care; Nairobi for complex needs.
best_for: Gorilla and wildlife trekking enthusiasts (Bwindi 6 hrs)
NGO and development sector retirees
Those wanting affordable equatorial African city life
avoid_if: Those needing specialist healthcare locally
Retirees wanting beach or coastal living

cities.description: Africa's cleanest and most organized capital — Kigali is a revelation of clean streets, zero plastic bags, efficient services, and a remarkably safe environment underpinned by extraordinary national resilience.
cities.visa_notes: Rwanda's 5-year Residency Permit for investors or those with income proof is available. Kigali is increasingly positioning itself as an international hub.
cities.healthcare_notes: King Faisal Hospital and Clinic Galien provide solid private care; Nairobi for advanced specialists.
best_for: Safety-above-all retirees wanting Africa's safest capital
Gorilla trekking enthusiasts (Volcanoes NP 2 hrs)
Those inspired by Rwanda's extraordinary transformation story
avoid_if: Budget retirees (Kigali is pricier than comparable African cities)
Those wanting beach or coastal access

cities.description: Africa's diplomatic capital at 2,355m — home to the African Union and UN Economic Commission for Africa, with extraordinary Ethiopian cuisine, ancient church culture, and improving infrastructure.
cities.visa_notes: Ethiopia's e-visa available; long-stay residence through investment or employment. No formal retirement visa; current political tensions require monitoring.
cities.healthcare_notes: Korean Hospital and some private clinics in Bole area; Nairobi for serious specialist cases.
best_for: Ethiopian history and culture enthusiasts
Coffee lovers (birthplace of coffee)
NGO and African Union sector retirees
avoid_if: Those concerned about political stability (regional conflicts)
Retirees needing reliable internet or healthcare

cities.description: West Africa's most livable capital — a stable democracy, strong English language, vibrant music and arts scene, and the 'Year of Return' diaspora program drawing African-American retirees.
cities.visa_notes: Ghana's Right of Abode (for African diaspora) and ECOWAS freedom of movement programs make it particularly accessible. Indefinite Residence Permit available.
cities.healthcare_notes: Nyaho Medical Centre and Trust Hospital provide solid private care; Accra has the best medical infrastructure in West Africa.
best_for: African-American and Caribbean diaspora retirees seeking ancestral connection
Those wanting a stable, English-speaking West African base
Music and culture enthusiasts (Afrobeats, highlife)
avoid_if: Those sensitive to heat and humidity
Retirees wanting advanced specialist care without travel

cities.description: The Ashanti cultural capital — Kumasi's Kejetia Market (Africa's largest), Kente weaving traditions, and rich royal heritage make it Ghana's most culturally distinctive city.
cities.visa_notes: Ghana residency programs apply. Kumasi is the heart of Akan culture and draws diaspora visitors interested in heritage.
cities.healthcare_notes: Komfo Anokye Teaching Hospital (largest in Ghana outside Accra) and private clinics.
best_for: Ashanti heritage and Kente culture enthusiasts
Budget-conscious diaspora retirees
Those wanting deep Ghanaian cultural immersion
avoid_if: Those wanting Accra's international sophistication
Retirees wanting a significant expat community

cities.description: Africa's largest city and its most dynamic — an overwhelming, extraordinary megacity of 20+ million people, incredible food, music, art, and commerce, at a premium cost of living for Africa.
cities.visa_notes: Nigeria's Subject of Commonwealth Citizenship or investment-based residency are the main long-stay pathways. Process can be bureaucratic.
cities.healthcare_notes: Eko Hospital and Reddington Hospital provide adequate private care; medical tourism to UK, Dubai, or India for serious cases.
best_for: Those with Nigerian family ties or business connections
Culture, music, and Afrobeats enthusiasts
Retirees willing to embrace megacity intensity
avoid_if: Safety-sensitive retirees (security requires significant precautions)
Anyone not comfortable with unpredictability and infrastructure challenges

cities.description: Nigeria's planned federal capital — significantly safer and more orderly than Lagos, with government-sector expat infrastructure, beautiful Aso Rock scenery, and wide boulevards.
cities.visa_notes: Nigeria residency applies. Abuja is the preferred base for diplomats, UN staff, and government-connected foreign residents.
cities.healthcare_notes: Cedarcrest Hospital and National Hospital Abuja provide the better private care options.
best_for: Those with diplomatic, government, or NGO Nigeria connections
Those preferring an ordered, planned Nigerian city over Lagos chaos
avoid_if: Those wanting cultural vibrancy (duller than Lagos)
Retirees without existing Nigeria ties

cities.description: West Africa's cultural capital on the Atlantic — extraordinary Senegalese cuisine, vibrant music (mbalax), the legendary Gorée Island, and one of the continent's most welcoming societies.
cities.visa_notes: Senegal allows 90-day visa-free stays for many nationalities; long-term residence via investment or work permit. Relatively accessible and stable.
cities.healthcare_notes: Clinique du Cap and Clinique Pasteur provide adequate private care; medical evacuations to France for serious cases.
best_for: Francophone retirees
Music (mbalax) and food culture enthusiasts
Those wanting Atlantic West African living with Francophone sophistication
avoid_if: Those sensitive to heat and humidity
Retirees needing specialist healthcare locally

cities.description: West Africa's economic powerhouse and most cosmopolitan city — excellent French-influenced cuisine, solid infrastructure, and a booming economy make it the region's most livable major city.
cities.visa_notes: Côte d'Ivoire requires a visa for most nationalities; long-stay residence via investment. ECOWAS residents have freedom of movement.
cities.healthcare_notes: Clinique Biasa and Polyclinique Internationale Sainte Anne-Marie provide quality French-standard private care.
best_for: Francophone West Africa business and culture lovers
Those wanting the most modern West African city infrastructure
French cuisine enthusiasts
avoid_if: Those sensitive to tropical heat
Non-French speakers (very limited English)

cities.description: Cameroon's peaceful highland capital — French-English bilingual, surrounded by seven hills and greenery, and offering Africa's most diverse culinary landscape in a stable central African setting.
cities.visa_notes: Cameroon requires a visa; residence is possible via investment. Regional tensions (Anglophone conflict in northwest/southwest) require awareness.
cities.healthcare_notes: Chantal Biya Foundation Hospital and Clinique la Caducée provide adequate private care; Abidjan or Paris for serious cases.
best_for: French-English bilingual Africa enthusiasts
Central Africa wildlife access seekers (Dja Faunal Reserve)
Those with diplomatic or NGO Cameroon connections
avoid_if: Those monitoring the Anglophone crisis in western regions
Retirees needing reliable internet or advanced healthcare

cities.description: Cameroon's economic and port capital — a bustling, chaotic commercial hub with the Wouri River estuary, excellent food, and the best business infrastructure in Central Africa.
cities.visa_notes: Cameroon visa and residence applies. Douala is preferred over Yaoundé by business-linked expats.
cities.healthcare_notes: Polyclinique la Croix du Sud and Clinique des Érables provide adequate private care.
best_for: Those with business or trade ties to Central Africa
Food enthusiasts (Cameroonian cuisine is extraordinary)
Francophone expats comfortable with tropical conditions
avoid_if: Those sensitive to intense heat and humidity
Retirees wanting quality of life over commercial energy

cities.description: A small, oil-wealthy Central African capital on the Atlantic with relatively good infrastructure, rainforest access, and Gabon's exceptional wildlife (gorillas, forest elephants, surfing hippos).
cities.visa_notes: Gabon's e-visa is available; residence via investment or employment. French-speaking only in government and formal contexts.
cities.healthcare_notes: Centre Hospitalier Universitaire de Libreville and private Clinique El Rapha provide adequate care for a Central African capital.
best_for: Wildlife and rainforest enthusiasts (gorilla, elephant, surfing hippo encounters)
Francophone retirees wanting Central African base
Those wanting oil-economy infrastructure in Central Africa
avoid_if: Non-French speakers
Those sensitive to tropical heat and cost levels
Retirees wanting specialist healthcare locally

cities.description: A charmingly faded Art Deco capital on Maputo Bay — brilliant piri-piri prawns, sidewalk cafés, Inhaca Island day trips, and a relaxed pace at very low cost.
cities.visa_notes: Mozambique's DIRE (residence permit) for investment or pension income is available. Portuguese language is a significant practical requirement.
cities.healthcare_notes: Clínica de Sommerschield and South African Medical Services clinic provide adequate private care; Johannesburg for specialist needs.
best_for: Portuguese speakers
Seafood and café culture lovers
Those wanting a charming, affordable Southern African coastal capital
avoid_if: Non-Portuguese speakers wanting services in English (limited)
Those needing reliable healthcare

cities.description: Mozambique's second port city — a gateway to the Zambezi Valley and Gorongosa National Park, with Indian Ocean beaches and ultra-low prices, though still recovering from cyclone damage.
cities.visa_notes: Mozambique DIRE applies. Beira's infrastructure is still recovering from Cyclone Idai (2019).
cities.healthcare_notes: Hospital Central da Beira provides public care; very limited private options — Maputo or Harare for meaningful care.
best_for: Gorongosa and Zambezi wildlife enthusiasts
Budget retirees comfortable with very limited infrastructure
Adventure-oriented self-reliant expats
avoid_if: Almost all retirees — healthcare and infrastructure limitations are significant

cities.description: Africa's cleanest and most orderly capital — a German colonial city surrounded by Namibia's extraordinary desert landscapes, with excellent infrastructure and a very low crime rate by African standards.
cities.visa_notes: Namibia's Retirement Visa requires proof of $1,500/month income. One of Africa's more accessible retirement visa programs.
cities.healthcare_notes: Roman Catholic Hospital and Mediclinic Windhoek provide solid private care.
best_for: Desert and wildlife enthusiasts (Sossusvlei, Etosha proximity)
Those wanting an orderly, very clean African capital
German speakers (unique German cultural heritage)
avoid_if: Those wanting warm, humid climate (Windhoek is dry and altitude-cool)
Retirees wanting beach access (landlocked, coast 4 hrs)

cities.description: The most surreal town in Africa — German colonial bakeries and beer halls sit alongside Atlantic fog, Namib Desert dunes, and whale-watching in a genuinely unique coastal setting.
cities.visa_notes: Namibia Retirement Visa applies. A favourite of South African and German retirees for its unique character.
cities.healthcare_notes: Welwitschia Hospital provides adequate care; Windhoek (3 hrs) for specialist needs.
best_for: Those wanting Africa's most unique town setting
Sand-dune and desert adventure enthusiasts
German speakers wanting a German-feel African town
avoid_if: Those wanting warm beach weather (Atlantic fog keeps it cool)
Retirees needing nearby specialist healthcare

cities.description: Africa's fastest-growing city and diamond capital — a modern, stable, and corruption-free planned city with a high standard of living by African measures and close access to Chobe and the Okavango.
cities.visa_notes: Botswana Residence Permit via investment or pension proof. One of Africa's most corruption-free and well-governed countries.
cities.healthcare_notes: Gaborone Private Hospital and Bokamoso Private Hospital provide solid care; Johannesburg (4 hrs) for specialists.
best_for: Those valuing political stability and good governance above all
Okavango Delta and Chobe safari enthusiasts
South Africa proximity with less crime
avoid_if: Those wanting coastal or tropical climate
Retirees wanting a vibrant cultural or entertainment scene

cities.description: Zambia's sprawling capital — a pleasant highland climate, good private schools, an English-speaking environment, and proximity to Victoria Falls and South Luangwa National Park.
cities.visa_notes: Zambia's Residence Permit for retirees requires income proof. English as the official language is a significant advantage for Anglophone retirees.
cities.healthcare_notes: Medi-Clinic Lusaka and Levy Mwanawasa Hospital for private care; Johannesburg for specialist needs.
best_for: English-speaking Africa enthusiasts
Victoria Falls and South Luangwa safari proximity
Those wanting Southern African wildlife access at moderate cost
avoid_if: Those needing specialist medical care
Retirees wanting a well-developed expat social scene

cities.description: The adventure capital of Africa, at the doorstep of Victoria Falls — white-water rafting, bungee jumping, game parks, and the world's most spectacular waterfall as a permanent backdrop.
cities.visa_notes: Zambia Residence Permit applies. Zimbabwe's Victoria Falls town is accessible via bridge.
cities.healthcare_notes: Livingstone Central Hospital and small private clinics; Lusaka (7 hrs) or Johannesburg for specialist needs.
best_for: Adventure and outdoors retirees wanting the world's greatest waterfall at their doorstep
Safari and wildlife enthusiasts
Active, self-reliant retirees
avoid_if: Those needing healthcare access
Retirees wanting urban amenities or nightlife

cities.description: Zimbabwe's tree-lined highland capital — once called the 'Sunshine City', with colonial jacaranda boulevards, a strong arts and cuisine scene, and a resilient expat community despite economic challenges.
cities.visa_notes: Zimbabwe's residency programs are available but require navigating currency instability and economic uncertainty. USD is widely used for daily transactions.
cities.healthcare_notes: Avenues Clinic and Parirenyatwa Hospital for private/public care; Johannesburg for specialist needs.
best_for: Those with existing Zimbabwe ties or connections
Resilient retirees comfortable with economic uncertainty
Those attracted to Harare's highland climate and artistic community
avoid_if: Retirees needing financial certainty (Zimbabwe's economic history is volatile)
Those needing reliable utilities (power outages common)

cities.description: The town built around the 'Smoke That Thunders' — a tourist and wildlife hub where elephant, buffalo, and hippo regularly wander through town, and the falls roar year-round.
cities.visa_notes: Zimbabwe residency applies. UNIVISA allows easy entry to both Zimbabwe and Zambia sides.
cities.healthcare_notes: Victoria Falls Hospital; Livingstone (Zambia, 10 min) or Bulawayo for more advanced care.
best_for: Adventure-devoted wildlife retirees
Those wanting elephants and the world's largest waterfall as neighbours
Active, self-reliant expats
avoid_if: Those needing healthcare infrastructure
Budget retirees (tourist pricing applies to everything)

cities.description: The hilltop capital of the world's most unique island — lemurs, baobabs, and extraordinary biodiversity in a city of rice paddies and highland markets, at very low cost.
cities.visa_notes: Madagascar allows 90-day tourist visas extendable; long-term residency requires investment. Unique island with very limited international connections.
cities.healthcare_notes: Clinique Adventiste Fiadanana provides some private care; medical evacuations to Réunion or Johannesburg for serious cases.
best_for: Naturalists and biodiversity enthusiasts
Adventurous Francophone retirees
Those wanting the world's most unique island culture on a budget
avoid_if: Those needing healthcare access or reliable internet
Non-French speakers
Retirees wanting any established expat community

cities.description: Madagascar's premier beach island — ylang-ylang-scented air, lemur encounters, whale shark diving, and beautiful bays at very affordable prices on the Indian Ocean.
cities.visa_notes: Madagascar visa situation applies. Island living with limited connections to the mainland.
cities.healthcare_notes: Very limited — local clinic only; evacuations to Antananarivo or Réunion.
best_for: Beach and marine biodiversity devotees
Adventurous retirees wanting Indian Ocean island life at low cost
Ylang-ylang and spice island culture enthusiasts
avoid_if: Those with any healthcare needs — evacuation required for anything serious
Those needing reliable internet or modern amenities

cities.description: The capital of the Indian Ocean's least-visited archipelago — a volcanic island of wild beaches, ancient medina streets, and unspoiled nature at true frontier prices.
cities.visa_notes: Comoros allows visa-on-arrival for most nationalities. No retirement visa; long stays are very difficult to arrange formally.
cities.healthcare_notes: El-Maarouf Hospital is severely under-resourced; any serious health issue requires emergency evacuation.
best_for: Extreme frontier adventurers wanting one of the world's most remote and unspoiled island nations
avoid_if: Almost all retirees — healthcare, infrastructure, and connectivity are among Africa's most limited

cities.description: The world's smallest capital, on the main island of Mahé — granite boulders, turquoise lagoons, coco de mer palms, and the most exclusive Indian Ocean retirement address.
cities.visa_notes: Seychelles' Gainful Occupation Permit or Non-Citizen Property Purchase allow residency. No income or capital gains tax. Cost of living is very high.
cities.healthcare_notes: Seychelles Hospital and private clinics adequate for routine care; Réunion or Johannesburg for specialist needs.
best_for: Ultra-premium Indian Ocean living
Those wanting zero income tax in paradise
Nature and marine conservation enthusiasts (UNESCO biosphere)
avoid_if: All non-affluent retirees — Seychelles is among the world's most expensive island nations

cities.description: The main island of the Seychelles archipelago — 65 granite islands of extraordinary natural beauty, Morne Seychellois National Park trails, and the finest beaches in the Indian Ocean.
cities.visa_notes: Seychelles residency options as per Victoria entry. Mahé is the only island with meaningful infrastructure.
cities.healthcare_notes: Same as Victoria — Seychelles Hospital and private clinics.
best_for: Those wanting to live on the main Seychelles island with access to all infrastructure
Hikers and nature lovers (Morne Seychellois)
Premium Indian Ocean lifestyle devotees
avoid_if: Budget retirees
Those needing advanced medical care

cities.description: The second-largest Seychelles island — home to Anse Lazio (perennially ranked among the world's most beautiful beaches) and the Vallée de Mai UNESCO World Heritage coco de mer forest.
cities.visa_notes: Seychelles residency applies. Praslin is quieter and more exclusive than Mahé, with ferry access.
cities.healthcare_notes: Very limited — small health centre only; ferry to Mahé for hospital care.
best_for: Those wanting one of the world's most beautiful beach island settings at any price
Coco de mer and endemic nature enthusiasts
Premium solitude seekers
avoid_if: Those needing healthcare access
Those wanting urban amenities or entertainment

cities.description: Mauritius's multicultural capital — Indian, African, Chinese, and French heritage blended in a port city with colorful markets, excellent street food, and the island's best hospital infrastructure.
cities.visa_notes: Mauritius's Retired Non-Citizen permit requires $1,500/month income transfer. One of Africa/Indian Ocean's best retirement visa programs. Also offers Premium Visa for 1-year renewable stays.
cities.healthcare_notes: City Clinic Wellkin and C-Care Wellkin are among the Indian Ocean's finest private hospitals. Medical tourism hub.
best_for: Those wanting the Indian Ocean's best retirement visa program
Multicultural food and culture enthusiasts
Health-conscious retirees wanting excellent Indian Ocean private hospitals
avoid_if: Those wanting beach-side living (Port Louis itself is inland-urban)
Those wanting a quiet retreat (Port Louis is a busy commercial city)

cities.description: Mauritius's most popular expat beach village on the calm western lagoon — white sand, a gentle shallow reef, spectacular sunsets, and a thriving European retiree community.
cities.visa_notes: Mauritius Retired Non-Citizen Permit or Premium Visa applies. Flic en Flac is the most established expat beach community on the island.
cities.healthcare_notes: Clinique de l'Ouest (nearby) and C-Care Wellkin (30 min) provide solid private care.
best_for: Beach retirees wanting the Indian Ocean's most developed expat community
Those wanting Mauritius's best western-lagoon sunsets
European retirees wanting an English/French-friendly island paradise
avoid_if: Budget retirees (Mauritius commands a quality premium)
Those wanting authentic local Mauritian immersion (heavily expat-oriented)

cities.description: Mauritius's northern resort hub — the island's most vibrant dining and nightlife scene, catamaran sailing, water sports, duty-free shopping, and a lively expat social calendar.
cities.visa_notes: Mauritius Retired Non-Citizen Permit or Premium Visa applies. Grand Baie has the island's most active social and dining scene for expats.
cities.healthcare_notes: Clinique du Nord nearby; Wellkin hospital (45 min south) for comprehensive care.
best_for: Active social retirees wanting Mauritius's best restaurant and entertainment scene
Water sports enthusiasts
Those wanting northern Mauritius's calmer, less humid climate
avoid_if: Those wanting peaceful, quiet beach living (Grand Baie can be busy)
Budget retirees
